description: Distill README, docs, and wiki around the validated fresh-machine setup flow (issue #559). Docs-only. Acceptance criteria use grep gates, content-presence checks, and wiki lint in place of typecheck/tests (no code changes). Revised after critique r1 (5 highs addressed): US-003 Railway/curl retention + Option-letter cross-ref, US-007 sandbox-only gateway, US-008 6-site scope + preserve-check, plus AC-precision mediums.
executionNotes: Build STRICTLY in priority order. US-001..US-008 are the canonical-fact edits; US-009 (walkthrough) and US-010 (wiki) are a FINAL SERIAL PASS that must run only after US-001..US-008 have landed, because both consume the final doc state. Do not fan out US-009/US-010 in parallel with earlier stories. Live-verification environment for US-006/US-007 is THIS sandbox (docker exec into the openharness container / run gateway status); 'provisional' in prose docs = an explicit '> Unverified — ...' blockquote admonition, never the wiki 'confidence:' field.

userStories.title: Fix host prerequisites to include make/build-essential
userStories.description: As a new operator, I need the docs to list make (build-essential) as a host prerequisite so make sandbox / make shell work on a fresh host.
acceptanceCriteria: installation.md Prerequisites lists Docker (+Compose), Git, and make/build-essential with the why (make sandbox / make shell)
README.md:7 and README.md:38 no longer say 'Docker + Git' only — they include make or point to the prereqs section
quickstart.md and intro.md prereq lines include make (or link to installation.md)
grep -rn 'Docker + Git' README.md .oh/docs/ (DIRECTORY arg, true recursion) returns 0 hits
Markdown renders (no broken tables/links introduced)
userStories.notes: Completed on 2026-07-02; host prereqs now include make across installation.md (canonical table), README, quickstart, intro

userStories.title: Add harness.yaml git: key reference; collapse README Configure prose to a pointer
userStories.description: As an operator, I need the harness.yaml config reference to document the git.user_name / git.user_email keys so I can set my identity, with a single canonical home.
acceptanceCriteria: quickstart.md config table documents git.user_name and git.user_email (allowlisted per harness-config.sh:63-64) and notes crons:/autopilot:/slack: sections exist
Config reference notes the file is harness.yaml and changes apply via 'make destroy && make sandbox'
README 'Configure (optional)' section (README.md:107-125 — it is PROSE, not a table) shrinks to a 2-sentence pointer to the quickstart config table; the secrets-live-in-.env warning is preserved
No config key contradicts .oh/scripts/harness-config.sh
userStories.notes: Completed on 2026-07-02; git.* keys added to quickstart config example+table; README Configure shrunk to pointer, secrets warning kept

userStories.title: Document the private-origin + upstream clone-and-own pattern (retain Railway/curl)
userStories.description: As an operator, I need the validated private-origin + upstream flow (gh repo create --private, SSH remotes, upstream to mifunedev) so I can reproduce steps 6-7 from the docs alone — WITHOUT losing the existing Railway/curl install paths.
acceptanceCriteria: installation.md has an enriched 'clone and own' section covering: gh repo create --private <user>/openharness, SSH remote URLs (git@github.com:...), git remote add upstream git@github.com:mifunedev/openharness.git

NO 4TH OPTION: fold the private-origin+upstream content into the EXISTING Option C (README.md:49-55 'Clone upstream, then re-point to your repo' — the nearest match) and enrich it, rather than adding a new lettered option; then promote/lead with it. This avoids 4 overlapping install paths, honoring 'one canonical home per fact'.
README Install section leads with (or clearly links) this validated private-origin+upstream path; Railway/curl remain as alternatives

REVERSIBLE + NON-DESTRUCTIVE: the Railway one-click button (railway.com button image + link) and the Option A curl one-liner (curl -fsSL https://oh.mifune.dev/install.sh | bash) must both remain PRESENT and functionally unchanged — only reordered/demoted in position, never deleted. Verify the FULL markdown block survives (Railway: the [![Deploy on Railway](...button.svg)](...) image+link; curl: the exact fenced command), not just the bare substring.

After any Option relabeling, re-run grep -n 'Option [A-Z]' README.md and confirm every letter reference still points to the same content — specifically README.md:154 'fork it (see Option B above)' must be re-verified and updated (or replaced with a descriptive/anchor reference) so it is not left dangling
A reader can reproduce flow steps 6-7 from installation.md alone; all new git/gh commands are copy-paste runnable and use SSH URLs consistently
userStories.notes: Completed on 2026-07-02; installation.md Clone-and-own enriched with private gh repo create + SSH remotes + upstream; README Option C enriched + Recommended lead callout; Railway/curl retained; Option-letter refs coherent

userStories.title: Document GitHub SSH auth path in integrations/github.md
userStories.description: As an operator, I need the interactive gh auth login SSH path and the entrypoint auto-keygen behavior documented so step 5 is reproducible.
acceptanceCriteria: integrations/github.md documents interactive 'gh auth login' with SSH protocol: generate SSH key, paste token
Documents the entrypoint auto-keygen behavior (entrypoint.sh:275-309: GH_TOKEN + admin:public_key scope uploads a key)
Cross-links the installation.md private-origin+upstream section (US-003)
Existing PAT/HTTPS content is preserved, not deleted
userStories.notes: Completed on 2026-07-02; github.md gains an SSH auth section (interactive gh auth login SSH path + entrypoint auto-keygen 275-309, admin:public_key scope), cross-linked to installation.md clone-and-own

userStories.title: Verify and correct the Claude auth command
userStories.description: As an operator, I need the real Claude auth command so step 8 works. Verify in the live sandbox before editing.
acceptanceCriteria: Claude auth command verified in THIS live sandbox (docker exec into the openharness container; run claude --help / inspect auth subcommands)
harnesses/claude-code.md states the verified command (claude auth login vs bare claude OAuth)
If genuinely unverifiable live, the doc flags it with an explicit '> Unverified — ...' blockquote admonition (NOT the wiki confidence: field), rather than asserting an unconfirmed command
Doc edit is made only after the verification attempt
userStories.notes: Completed on 2026-07-02; verified live in openharness container (Claude Code v2.1.198): claude auth login/status/logout are real subcommands; fixed claude-code.md + quickstart harness list to use claude auth login

userStories.title: Align Slack/gateway docs and add a read-only Run-and-verify subsection (sandbox-only)
userStories.description: As an operator, I need slack.md and hermes.md to agree on the gateway architecture and teach me how to run and verify the gateways without accidentally killing them.
acceptanceCriteria: hermes.md and slack.md are reconciled to gateway.sh ground truth: shared lifecycle script (.oh/scripts/gateway.sh) in sibling tmux sessions client-slack-pi and client-slack-hermes, each with its own independent Slack app/config

SANDBOX-ONLY: the run commands are documented as sandbox-only — both 'gateway <pi|hermes>' and 'make gateway <pi|hermes>' require pi/hermes on PATH (gateway.sh:64-65,118-119 error 'run inside the sandbox' otherwise). Do NOT document 'make gateway on host'. Match the existing slack.md:141-142 framing.
BOTH docs gain a 'Run and verify' subsection: start (gateway <pi|hermes> or make gateway <pi|hermes>, in the sandbox), status (gateway status)
Verify read-only: tmux attach -r -t client-slack-pi / tmux attach -r -t client-slack-hermes
Detach once validated: Ctrl-b d (never Ctrl-C/exit); logs at /tmp/client-slack-pi.log and /tmp/client-slack-hermes.log

gateway status output / session naming verified against the live sandbox; if no gateway session is running, state so and mark the attach example with a '> Unverified — ...' admonition rather than the 'where possible' hedge
userStories.notes: Completed on 2026-07-02; slack.md +4.6 Run-and-verify (read-only), observation attaches -> -r; hermes.md gateway section reconciled to shared gateway.sh lifecycle + Run-and-verify. gateway status format and tmux -r flag live-verified (tmux 3.3a)

userStories.title: Remove unused ryaneggz/mifune repo references (6 named sites only)
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the never-used ryaneggz/mifune repo references removed so docs point to pi-messenger-bridge / slack.md instead — without scrubbing historical record.
acceptanceCriteria: SCOPE: edit ONLY the 6 named line-sites — README.md:10, README.md:137, intro.md:19, contributing.md:78, harnesses/overview.md:9, AGENTS.md:80. NO repo-wide find/replace.
README.md:10 and README.md:137 re-point multi-agent messaging to pi-messenger-bridge + integrations/slack.md
intro.md:19 re-points to pi-messenger-bridge / slack.md

contributing.md 'Installing a harness pack' subsection: rewrite the MECHANISM, not just the link — the current text (git clone <pack-repo> workspace/<pack>; '@ryaneggz/mifune defines the pack contract') is factually wrong for pi-messenger-bridge (which is npm install --prefix .pi/bridge, loaded via pi --extension per gateway.sh:82-84). Rewrite to describe the real bridge install (or generalize honestly) and drop the false 'pack contract' claim
harnesses/overview.md:9 drops the pack mention
AGENTS.md:80 drops the 'legacy pack still works' sentence (CLAUDE.md is a symlink, one edit)

The generic 'harness packs' extensibility concept (e.g. installation.md:245 compose.overrides) is OUT of scope — only the ryaneggz/mifune-named pack-contract example is retired; do not touch generic pack/extension prose
grep -rn 'ryaneggz/mifune' README.md AGENTS.md .oh/docs/ returns 0 hits

PRESERVE-CHECK (PER-PATH, not aggregate): the archived task folder is now .oh/tasks/archive/2026-07-02/mifune-repo-extraction/ (the live .oh/tasks/mifune-repo-extraction/ was archived by the cleanup-tasks cron). Baselines to hold UNCHANGED: CHANGELOG.md = 14 hits, .oh/tasks/archive/2026-07-02/mifune-repo-extraction/ = 64 hits (grep -rc ryaneggz/mifune per path). CHANGELOG.md is the git-tracked audit trail; the archive is gitignored working-tree history.
userStories.notes: Completed on 2026-07-02; removed ryaneggz/mifune from the 6 named sites; contributing pack-mechanism rewritten to pi-messenger-bridge; preserve-check held (CHANGELOG 14, archive 64 unchanged)

userStories.title: Add the end-to-end 13-step quickstart walkthrough (self-sufficient)
userStories.description: As a new operator, I need one ordered start-to-finish narrative I can execute top-to-bottom so I go bare-host to authed sandbox without hunting across docs.
acceptanceCriteria: FINAL SERIAL PASS: implement only after US-001..US-008 have landed (consumes their final doc state)
quickstart.md contains an ordered 13-step walkthrough matching the validated flow

SELF-SUFFICIENT: each step INLINES the actual command(s) to run (not just a link), so the walkthrough alone is copy-paste runnable; canonical docs (installation.md, github.md, harnesses/*.md, debugmcp.md, slack.md, hermes.md) are linked as supplementary depth/troubleshooting, not required reading to finish setup
FORMAT BOUND: keep each step's inlined command block tight (~1-3 lines); push troubleshooting/depth to the linked canonical doc, honoring USER.md 'concise and action-oriented'

This inlining is a DELIBERATE, NAMED exception to 'one canonical home per fact' (recorded in prd.md architecture note): quickstart = the operator read-path; canonical docs = authoritative depth. A drift probe is a listed optional fast-follow, not this PR.
The walkthrough is internally consistent with US-001..US-008 edits; no command contradicts its canonical doc
Every inlined command also appears in / agrees with its canonical doc
userStories.notes: Completed on 2026-07-02; replaced quickstart Next-steps with a self-sufficient 13-step end-to-end walkthrough, each step inlining commands and linking its canonical doc; links verified to resolve

userStories.title: Author the fresh-machine-setup wiki entity page
userStories.description: As an agent, I need an LLM-readable synthesis of the setup flow in the wiki corpus so future sessions recall it via /wiki query — without becoming a drifting second copy of the human quickstart.
acceptanceCriteria: FINAL SERIAL PASS: implement after US-009 (references quickstart.md as the canonical human walkthrough)
.oh/skills/wiki/corpus/fresh-machine-setup.md exists and follows .oh/skills/wiki/references/schema.md (frontmatter title/slug/tags/created/updated/sources/related/confidence; ordered sections)

sources: cites the touched in-repo doc/source files (installation.md, integrations/github.md, .oh/scripts/gateway.sh, etc.) per the sandbox-dependency-installs.md precedent — NOT a raw/ snapshot (this is first-hand synthesis, no ingested URL)

NO LITERAL-COMMAND DUPLICATION (drift-surface removal): the page holds the synthesis + a Mermaid diagram mapping the 13 steps to their canonical doc handoffs; it does NOT reproduce the literal shell commands — quickstart.md is named as the command source. This makes 'keep in sync' near-vacuous because there is no command-level content to drift.
Body <=900 words; explicitly names quickstart.md as the canonical human walkthrough
confidence: provisional on any step not verified live; created/updated set to the build date (UTC)
Entry whitelisted with git add -f (corpus is gitignored-by-default); .oh/skills/wiki/corpus/README.md index regenerated to include the new row (sorted by updated desc)
bash .oh/evals/probes/wiki-readme-index.sh passes and /wiki lint reports no broken links
